R0036.description: Light of Hope / 2026 / 30"x40" / Acrylic on Canvas
R0036.descEn:
“By his wounds we are healed.” — Isaiah 53:5

Theme

This painting shows a cluster of weathered stones glowing quietly under a warm light. Each stone carries dents and scars, marks left by years of wear, by circumstance, by life pressing against it again and again. But when the light falls from above, the scars are no longer just scars. They become the very places best able to catch the light.

Artist's Note

What I wanted to express is the moment when suffering and hope exist together.

A stone is by nature cold, silent, something trodden underfoot. Worn by water, ground down by sand, it loses its edges piece by piece. This mirrors a human life. We are all pressed and worn down by hardships of every kind. But scripture tells us that the Lord who bore the whipping, his wounds became the source of our healing. Suffering itself is not the end. Once suffering is touched by light, hope appears.

The blue violet shadows in the painting are the pain not yet passed. The warm gold light is grace coming from above. The sheen of water on the stone surface marks what has been washed clean and healed. I am not painting suffering. I am painting what suffering looks like after it has been touched by light.

I chose stone as my subject because it holds two meanings for me at once. Before faith, the human heart is hardened like stone, cold and unmoved. After faith, that same heart becomes just as firm, unshakable as a rock, standing faithful to the Lord through every storm without wavering. The same material carries the true face of both stages of the life of faith, and that is exactly why stone is the fitting way to express the inner journey of belief.

May everyone who is suffering see, in this light, that the wounds you carry are not the end. Hope is not the absence of suffering. It is the light shining above it.
R0036.descZh:
「因他受的鞭傷，我們得醫治。」——以賽亞書 53:5

主題

這幅畫畫的是一堆飽經磨蝕的石頭，在一道暖光中靜靜發亮。每一顆石頭都帶著凹痕與傷痕，那是被歲月、被環境、被生命反覆打磨的記號。但當光從上面照下來，傷痕不再只是傷痕，它們成了最能接住光的地方。

創作說明

我想表達的，是苦難與盼望同時存在的時刻。

石頭本是冰冷、沉默、被人踩在腳下的。它們被水沖、被沙磨，一塊一塊失去了稜角。這正像人的一生，我們都在各樣的患難裡被壓、被磨。但聖經說，那位被鞭傷的主，祂的傷成了我們得醫治的源頭。痛苦本身不是終點，痛苦被光照亮之後，就有了盼望。

畫面裡藍紫的陰影是還沒過去的苦楚，暖金的光是從上而來的恩典。石面上那一層水光，是被洗淨、被醫治的痕跡。我不是在畫痛苦，是在畫痛苦被光照亮之後的樣子。

我選擇以石頭為創作主題，是因為石頭在我心中有一體兩面的意義。信主之前，人的心如石頭一般剛硬，冷漠，不受感動；信主之後，這顆心卻同樣如石頭一般堅定，如磐石一樣不可動搖，任憑狂風暴雨無情摧殘，依然對主忠心不移。同一種材質，卻承載著信仰生命兩個階段的真實面貌，這正是石頭最適合表達內心信仰歷程的原因。

願每一個正在受苦的人，在這片光裡看見，你身上的傷不是終點，盼望不是苦難消失，而是苦難之上有光。
